question_text: A student looks at an object through a convex lens and sees an enlarged virtual image. Which of the following must be true about the object's position?

options.label: A
options.text: The object is at the focal point of the lens

options.label: B
options.text: The object is closer to the lens than the focal point

options.label: C
options.text: The object is much further than twice the focal length

options.label: D
options.text: The object is exactly twice the focal length away

explanation: When an object is placed closer to a convex lens than its focal point (within one focal length), it acts like a magnifying glass and produces an enlarged virtual image on the same side as the object. This is the principle used in magnifying glasses and reading lenses.
